rulings.Case Title: BETTY UTTERBACK VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Impress Lien And Application For Distribution Of Settlement Proceeds
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 4, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. County of Sacramento¿s motion to impress lien and application for distribution of settlement proceeds will be heard in Department 514. The parties should contact Department 514 to confirm the new hearing date and time. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: MARGARET PATRICK, et al VS. METROPOLITAN LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 4, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Chicago Bridge & Iron Company¿s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication: hearing required. The hearing will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: MARGARET PATRICK, et al VS. METROPOLITAN LIFE INSURANCE COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Leave To File First Amended Complaint;
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 4, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiffs¿ motion for order granting leave to file first amended complaint is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 473. Plaintiffs demonstrated that the pleadings should be amended to conform to the facts. There is no delay on the part of plaintiffs that warrants denial of the motion. Record v. Reason (1999) 73 Cal.App.4th 472 is distinguishable. As to the statute of limitations argument, the court does not normally consider the viability of a proposed claim. However, even if the court considers it, plaintiffs¿ proposed amended complaint which alleges the premises owner/contractor cause of action against Chicago Bridge & Iron Company relates back to the filing of the original complaint and is not barred by the statute of limitations. The cases relied upon by defendant, Barrington v. A.H. Robins Co. (1985) 39 Cal.3d 146 and Coronet Manufacturing Co., Inc. v. Superior Court (1979) 90 Cal.App.3d 342, are distinguishable. The First Amended Complaint in the form attached to plaintiffs¿ moving papers is deemed served on all defendants who have previously appeared herein as of the date granting this motion. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: PRATT, AVIS INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUCCESSOR-IN-INTER et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Plaintiffs Objection To Defendant Caesars Entertainment Corporations Request For Entry Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, April 4, 2013 in Department 503 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Hearing on plaintiffs¿ objection to specially appearing defendant Caesars Entertainment Corp.¿s proposed judgment: the court will not sign the judgment. The court granted Caesars¿ motion to quash and to dismiss action for lack of personal jurisdiction. Therefore this court does not have jurisdiction to enter judgment in favor of Caesars. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:45a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)
